published.text:
### Guides hub
Added a stronger “Start here” path by promoting the best-first-automation decision page into the featured guide set and adding a proof-hub callout:

- “Need proof before picking a first automation?”
- “Review the case-study hub to connect these buying guides to live automation outcomes before you choose a build sequence.”

### Case studies hub
Added a proof-to-decision linking block:

- “Connect proof to the decision pages”
- “Use these proof assets with the main automation guides”
- “These case studies support the pages Google has discovered but not crawled yet: the consultant overview, the best-first-automation guide, and the main guides hub.”

### AI automation consultant page
Added a “Continue researching” internal authority section before FAQ:

- “The next pages to read before choosing an automation project”
- “If you are still deciding what to automate first, use the guides hub, the best-first-automation decision page, and the proof hub together.”

### Best AI automations page
Added related links to:

- All automation guides
- Case studies
- AI automation consultant for small business

edits.description: This was a crawl-priority/internal-linking pass, not a net-new content page.
The copy stays practical and buyer-oriented: “start here,” “proof before picking,” “choosing an automation project.”
The link blocks are explicit for crawlers but still useful to humans deciding what to read next.
Avoided fake claims about indexing or results. The language says Google has discovered pages, not that Google has indexed or ranked them.
Kept proof references bounded to existing case-study assets and existing commercial/category pages.
